title: Starlight Festival Preparations
description: Help Mio prepare for the annual Starlight Festival

start.text: Oh! You're here! Perfect timing! The Starlight Festival is just three days away and I could really use an extra pair of hands. What do you say?

choices.text: I'd love to help! What needs doing?
Tell me about the festival first.
I'm not sure I'm qualified...

eager_help.text: That's the spirit! *claps hands* Okay, so we need to hang the lanterns along the main path, set up the food stalls, and most importantly - we need someone to test the fireworks safety zone. Interested?

choices.text: Lanterns sound beautiful. Let's start there!
Did you say fireworks?!
I'll help with whatever is most needed.

festival_info.text: Oh, you haven't heard? The Starlight Festival has been a Springtown tradition for over fifty years! My grandmother started it. Every summer, we light a thousand paper lanterns and release them into the sky. It's said that each lantern carries a wish. *eyes sparkle* It's the most magical night of the year.

choices.text: That sounds incredibly beautiful.
Your grandmother sounds like an amazing person.

reassure.text: Nonsense! The festival is for everyone! You don't need special skills - just a willing heart and maybe some comfortable shoes. *laughs* Trust me, by the end of the night, you'll feel like you've been part of Springtown forever.

choices.text: When you put it that way... count me in!
Comfortable shoes? How much walking is involved?

lanterns.text: Great choice! Each lantern is handmade by the townspeople. My grandmother believed that handcrafting the lanterns gave them a piece of your soul. *carefully picks up a lantern* Here, try this one. See how the paper catches the light? When we release them, it's like letting go of your worries and sending your hopes into the sky.

choices.text: Can I make one too?
What do you wish for every year?

fireworks.text: *grins* I knew you'd pick fireworks! We have a special display this year - Hiro helped design some new patterns. But we need to make sure the safety perimeter is clear. Want to help me check the field? *hands you a clipboard* Safety first, fun second... okay, maybe tied for first.

choices.text: Safety officer Mio at your service! Let's go.
Hiro made fireworks? That sounds... explosive.

humble.text: *beams* That's the most festival-ready answer I've heard all week! Okay, I'm putting you on lantern duty. The lanterns need to be hung at exactly the right height - not too low (fire hazard) and not too high (can't see the glow). Ready?

choices.text: Ready! Show me the way.

touched.text: *smiles softly* It really is. Last year, a little girl told me that watching the lanterns float away made her believe in magic again. That's why I do this - not for the big spectacle, but for those small moments. *pauses* Would you like to help create more of those moments?

choices.text: Absolutely. Where do I sign up?

grandmother.text: *eyes glisten* She really was. She taught me that the best festivals aren't about perfection - they're about bringing hearts together. I still use her original lantern patterns. *pulls out an old, worn sketchbook* See? Each design tells a story. Would you like to hear one?

choices.text: I'd love to hear every single one.
She must be very proud of you.

practical.text: *laughs* Fair question! The festival grounds stretch from the cherry blossom park all the way to the riverbank. That's about a fifteen-minute walk end to end. But trust me, with all the food stalls, games, and performances along the way, you won't even notice the distance!

choices.text: Food stalls? I'm sold!

make_lantern.text: Of course! *sets out materials* Here's the bamboo frame, the paper, and the glue. The trick is to be gentle - the paper is thin as a whisper. My grandmother always said 'treat each lantern like a letter to someone you love.' *watches you carefully* That's it... beautiful! Your first festival lantern!

choices.text: I'll write a wish on it and release it at the festival.

mio_wish.text: *blushes slightly* Every year I wish for the same thing - that the festival will keep bringing people together for another fifty years. That the lanterns will keep glowing. That Springtown will always feel like home. *looks up* What about you? What would you wish for?

choices.text: Right now? More moments like this one.

fireworks_check.text: *marches purposefully with clipboard* Okay, perimeter check! Left side clear... right side clear... *suddenly stops* Wait, is that a cat? *a small tabby cat sits in the middle of the field* Well, I guess we have our first official festival mascot! *scribbles on clipboard* 'Safety check: one adorable obstacle.'

choices.text: The cat clearly has great taste in festival locations.

hiro_fireworks.text: Right?! I was nervous at first, but Hiro's actually really careful with safety. He built a special launcher that keeps everything at a safe distance. *leans in* Between you and me, he's been working on a secret finale pattern. Even I haven't seen it yet!

choices.text: A secret finale? Now I'm definitely staying for the whole show!

stories.text: This one here - the spiral pattern - represents the journey of life. My grandmother drew it the year she first came to Springtown. And this one, the starburst, she designed for her first festival. *traces the patterns* Each lantern is a chapter of her story. And now... *hands you the book* ...I'd like you to be part of the next chapter.

choices.text: I'd be honored. Let's make this the best festival yet.

proud.text: *wipes eyes* Thank you. I... I really needed to hear that today. *takes a deep breath* You know what? You're good at this. The festival, the preparations - it's all better with someone like you here. Ready to get to work?

choices.text: Always. Let's make your grandmother proud.

ending_wish.text: That's beautiful! I'll make sure your lantern goes right in the center of the formation - the heart of the wish cluster. *carefully places your lantern* Three days from now, this little light will join a thousand others in the sky. And every single one will carry hope. Thank you for being part of this.

ending_together.text: *blushes deeper* That's... *laughs softly* You know, in all the years I've organized this festival, nobody's ever said something like that to me before. *looks at the lanterns* I have a feeling this is going to be the best Starlight Festival yet. I'm really glad you're here.

ending_cat.text: *laughs* You know what? The best festivals always have a little chaos. That's what makes them memorable! *scribbles on clipboard* Okay, safety check complete, one cat mascot acquired, and I have the best assistant in Springtown. Let's make this festival shine!

ending_best.text: *eyes shining* Yes! Let's do it! *grabs your hand* Come on, we've got lanterns to hang, fireworks to check, and a festival to build! *pauses at the door* And hey - thank you. For being here. For caring. It means more than you know. Now, festival time!
